data.summary: LangChain and LlamaIndex are two prominent frameworks for building applications with Large Language Models (LLMs), particularly in the context of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. LangChain is characterized as a versatile, modular platform supporting numerous use cases beyond RAG, including various natural language processing (NLP) and agentic AI applications. It excels in building complex workflows and offers a wide range of integrations. LlamaIndex, on the other hand, is more focus
gaps: Detailed performance metrics comparing LangChain and LlamaIndex across various use cases
Long-term maintenance and community support projections for both frameworks
Integration capabilities with emerging AI technologies beyond current LLMs
Specific industry adoption rates and success stories for each framework
